```{r plot_instability, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
         x = "Sample",
         y = "Expansion Ratio",
         fill = "Analysis Range") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p_ratio)
  ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "15_expansion_ratio.png"),
         p_ratio, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  

# NEW INSTABILITY PLOTS - INSERT AFTER LINE 1047 (after expansion ratio plot)

  # Plot 5: Contraction Ratio (control setpoint)
  p_contraction_ratio <- ggplot(ratio_data,
                                aes(x = file_stem, y = contraction_ratio, fill = analysis_range_name)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
    labs(title = "Contraction Ratio (Control Setpoint)",
         subtitle = "(Reads below control) / (Reads at control)",
         x = "Sample",
         y = "Contraction Ratio",
         fill = "Analysis Range") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p_contraction_ratio)
  ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "16_contraction_ratio.png"),
         p_contraction_ratio, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
  # Plot 6: Total Tail Ratio (both setpoint types)
  p_total_tail <- ggplot(instability_metrics,
                         aes(x = file_stem, y = total_tail_ratio, fill = analysis_range_name)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_grid(setpoint_type ~ group, scales = "free_x",
           labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
    labs(title = "Total Tail Ratio",
         subtitle = "(Reads above + Reads below) / Reads at setpoint",
         x = "Sample",
         y = "Total Tail Ratio",
         fill = "Analysis Range") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p_total_tail)
  ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "17_total_tail_ratio.png"),
         p_total_tail, width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  
  # Plot 7: Read Counts by Region (stacked bars)
  read_counts_data <- instability_metrics %>%
    select(file_stem, analysis_range_name, group, setpoint_type,
           reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint),
                 names_to = "region",
                 values_to = "count") %>%
    mutate(region = case_when(
      region == "reads_below_setpoint" ~ "Below setpoint",
      region == "reads_at_setpoint" ~ "At setpoint",
      region == "reads_above_setpoint" ~ "Above setpoint"
    ),
    # Set factor levels: below first (bottom), at middle, above last (top)
    # This matches legend order
    region = factor(region, levels = c("Below setpoint", "At setpoint", "Above setpoint")))
  
  p_read_counts <- ggplot(read_counts_data,
                          aes(x = file_stem, y = count, fill = region)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack", alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_grid(setpoint_type + analysis_range_name ~ group, scales = "free",
           labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
    labs(title = "Read Distribution by Region Relative to Setpoint",
         subtitle = "Absolute read counts below, at, and above setpoint",
         x = "Sample",
         y = "Number of Reads",
         fill = "Region") +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("Below setpoint" = "steelblue", 
                 "At setpoint" = "grey70",
                 "Above setpoint" = "coral"),
      # Explicit breaks to ensure legend order
      breaks = c("Below setpoint", "At setpoint", "Above setpoint")
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p_read_counts)
  ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "18_read_counts_by_region.png"),
         p_read_counts, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  
  # Plot 7b: Read Proportions by Region (stacked bars showing proportions)
  read_proportions_data <- instability_metrics %>%
    mutate(
      total_reads = reads_below_setpoint + reads_at_setpoint + reads_above_setpoint,
      prop_below = reads_below_setpoint / total_reads,
      prop_at = reads_at_setpoint / total_reads,
      prop_above = reads_above_setpoint / total_reads
    ) %>%
    select(file_stem, analysis_range_name, group, setpoint_type,
           prop_below, prop_at, prop_above) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(prop_below, prop_at, prop_above),
                 names_to = "region",
                 values_to = "proportion") %>%
    mutate(region = case_when(
      region == "prop_below" ~ "Below setpoint",
      region == "prop_at" ~ "At setpoint",
      region == "prop_above" ~ "Above setpoint"
    ),
    # Set factor levels: below first (bottom), at middle, above last (top)
    # This matches legend order
    region = factor(region, levels = c("Below setpoint", "At setpoint", "Above setpoint")))
  
  p_read_proportions <- ggplot(read_proportions_data,
                               aes(x = file_stem, y = proportion, fill = region)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack", alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_grid(setpoint_type + analysis_range_name ~ group, scales = "free_x",
           labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
    labs(title = "Read Distribution by Region Relative to Setpoint (Proportions)",
         subtitle = "Proportion of reads below, at, and above setpoint",
         x = "Sample",
         y = "Proportion of Reads",
         fill = "Region") +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("Below setpoint" = "steelblue", 
                 "At setpoint" = "grey70",
                 "Above setpoint" = "coral"),
      # Explicit breaks to ensure legend order
      breaks = c("Below setpoint", "At setpoint", "Above setpoint")
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p_read_proportions)
  ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "19_read_proportions_by_region.png"),
         p_read_proportions, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  
  # Plot 8: Expansion vs Contraction Balance (scatter)
  balance_data <- instability_metrics %>%
    filter(!is.na(contraction_ratio), !is.na(expansion_ratio))
  
  if (nrow(balance_data) > 0) {
    p_balance_scatter <- ggplot(balance_data,
                                aes(x = contraction_ratio, y = expansion_ratio, 
                                    colour = group, shape = analysis_range_name)) +
      geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
      geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7) +
      facet_wrap(~ setpoint_type, nrow = 1) +
      labs(title = "Expansion vs Contraction Balance",
           subtitle = "Dashed line = balanced (expansion = contraction). Points above = more expansion; below = more contraction",
           x = "Contraction Ratio (reads below / reads at setpoint)",
           y = "Expansion Ratio (reads above / reads at setpoint)",
           colour = "Group",
           shape = "Analysis Range") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position = "right")
    
    print(p_balance_scatter)
    ggsave(file.path(plots_dir, "20_expansion_contraction_balance.png"),
           p_balance_scatter, width = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  }
  

  # Generate individual plots by analysis range
  message("\nGenerating individual plots by analysis range...")
  
  instability_by_range_dir <- file.path(plots_dir, "instability_by_range")
  if (!dir.exists(instability_by_range_dir)) {
    dir.create(instability_by_range_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  for (my_range in unique(instability_metrics$analysis_range_name)) {
    range_data <- instability_metrics %>%
      filter(analysis_range_name == my_range)
    
    if (nrow(range_data) == 0) next
    
    # Plot: All instability indices for this range
    p_range <- ggplot(range_data,
                      aes(x = file_stem, y = instability_index, fill = setpoint_type)) +
      geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~ group, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("sample_relative" = "steelblue", 
                                   "control_relative" = "coral"),
                       labels = c("sample_relative" = "Relative to Sample",
                                 "control_relative" = "Relative to Control")) +
      labs(title = paste0("Instability Index - ", my_range),
           x = "Sample",
           y = "Instability Index",
           fill = "Setpoint Type") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            legend.position = "bottom")
    
    ggsave(file.path(instability_by_range_dir, 
                    paste0("instability_index-", my_range, ".png")),
           p_range, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    
    # Plot: Read counts by region for this range
    read_counts_range <- range_data %>%
      select(file_stem, group, setpoint_type,
             reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = c(reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint),
                   names_to = "region",
                   values_to = "count") %>%
      mutate(region = case_when(
        region == "reads_below_setpoint" ~ "Below setpoint",
        region == "reads_at_setpoint" ~ "At setpoint",
        region == "reads_above_setpoint" ~ "Above setpoint"
      ),
      # Set factor levels: above first so it stacks on TOP
      region = factor(region, levels = c("Above setpoint", "At setpoint", "Below setpoint")))
    
    p_counts_range <- ggplot(read_counts_range,
                             aes(x = file_stem, y = count, fill = region)) +
      geom_col(position = "stack", alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_grid(setpoint_type ~ group, scales = "free",
                 labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Below setpoint" = "steelblue", 
                                   "At setpoint" = "grey70",
                                   "Above setpoint" = "coral")) +
      labs(title = paste0("Read Counts by Region - ", my_range),
           x = "Sample",
           y = "Number of Reads",
           fill = "Region") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            legend.position = "bottom")
    
    ggsave(file.path(instability_by_range_dir, 
                    paste0("read_counts-", my_range, ".png")),
           p_counts_range, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  }
  
  message("  Individual range plots saved to: ", instability_by_range_dir)
  
  # Generate individual plots by sample
  message("\nGenerating individual plots by sample...")
  
  instability_by_sample_dir <- file.path(plots_dir, "instability_by_sample")
  if (!dir.exists(instability_by_sample_dir)) {
    dir.create(instability_by_sample_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  for (my_sample in unique(instability_metrics$file_stem)) {
    sample_data <- instability_metrics %>%
      filter(file_stem == my_sample)
    
    if (nrow(sample_data) == 0) next
    
    # Create a multi-panel plot for this sample
    # Panel 1: Instability indices
    indices_data <- sample_data %>%
      select(analysis_range_name, setpoint_type, 
             instability_index, expansion_index, contraction_index) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = c(instability_index, expansion_index, contraction_index),
                   names_to = "metric",
                   values_to = "value") %>%
      mutate(metric = gsub("_", " ", metric),
             metric = tools::toTitleCase(metric))
    
    p1 <- ggplot(indices_data,
                aes(x = analysis_range_name, y = value, fill = metric)) +
      geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
      facet_wrap(~ setpoint_type, nrow = 2,
                 labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
      labs(title = "Instability Indices",
           x = "Analysis Range",
           y = "Index Value",
           fill = "Metric") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    
    # Panel 2: Read counts
    counts_data <- sample_data %>%
      select(analysis_range_name, setpoint_type,
             reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = c(reads_below_setpoint, reads_at_setpoint, reads_above_setpoint),
                   names_to = "region",
                   values_to = "count") %>%
      mutate(region = case_when(
        region == "reads_below_setpoint" ~ "Below",
        region == "reads_at_setpoint" ~ "At",
        region == "reads_above_setpoint" ~ "Above"
      ),
      # Set factor levels: above first so it stacks on TOP
      region = factor(region, levels = c("Above", "At", "Below")))
    
    p2 <- ggplot(counts_data,
                aes(x = analysis_range_name, y = count, fill = region)) +
      geom_col(position = "stack", alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~ setpoint_type, nrow = 2,
                 labeller = labeller(setpoint_type = setpoint_labels)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Below" = "steelblue", 
                                   "At" = "grey70",
                                   "Above" = "coral")) +
      labs(title = "Read Distribution",
           x = "Analysis Range",
           y = "Number of Reads",
           fill = "Region") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    
    # Combine plots
    p_combined <- cowplot::plot_grid(p1, p2, ncol = 2,
                                     labels = c("A", "B"),
                                     label_size = 14)
    
    # Add title
    title_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() + 
      cowplot::draw_label(paste0("Instability Metrics - ", my_sample),
                         fontface = "bold", size = 16)
    
    p_final <- cowplot::plot_grid(title_text, p_combined, ncol = 1,
                                  rel_heights = c(0.05, 1))
    
    ggsave(file.path(instability_by_sample_dir, 
                    paste0(my_sample, "-instability.png")),
           p_final, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  }
  
  message("  Individual sample plots saved to: ", instability_by_sample_dir)

  message("\nInstability plots saved to: ", plots_dir)
}
```

---

# Export to Excel

Export all results to Excel workbook.

```{r export_excel, echo=FALSE}
# Prepare export list - comprehensive summary first
excel_export <- list(
  comprehensive_range_summary = comprehensive_range_summary,
  range_summary = range_summary,
  modal_peaks = modal_peaks,
  distribution_summary = distribution_summary
)

if (!is.null(group_controls)) {
  excel_export$group_controls <- group_controls
}

if (nrow(instability_metrics) > 0) {
  excel_export$instability_metrics <- instability_metrics
}

# Export to Excel
excel_path <- file.path(range_dir, "range_analysis.xlsx")
openxlsx::write.xlsx(excel_export, file = excel_path)

message("\nResults exported to Excel: ", excel_path)
message("  Sheets: ", paste(names(excel_export), collapse = ", "))
```

**Excel file contains:**

- **comprehensive_range_summary**: Complete table with all parameters, peaks, and controls (NEW!)
- **range_summary**: Analysis range definitions per sample
- **modal_peaks**: Detected peaks per sample and range
- **distribution_summary**: Full distribution statistics
- **group_controls**: Control setpoints per group (if enabled)
- **instability_metrics**: Instability metrics (if enabled)

---

# Summary

**Range analysis complete:**

- **`r length(unique(modal_peaks$file_stem))`** samples processed
- **`r nrow(modal_peaks)`** total peaks detected
- **`r nrow(distribution_summary)`** distribution summaries calculated

---

# Save outputs

```{r save_outputs, echo=FALSE}
# Compile module output
module6_output <- list(
  settings = settings_parsed,
  comprehensive_range_summary = comprehensive_range_summary,
  modal_peaks = modal_peaks,
  distribution_summary = distribution_summary,
  group_controls = group_controls,
  instability_metrics = instability_metrics
)

# Save complete module output
results_dir <- file.path(params$dir_out, "module_data")
if (!dir.exists(results_dir)) dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE)

output_path <- file.path(results_dir, "06_range_analysis_results.RData")
save(module6_output, file = output_path)

message("Module 6 outputs saved to: ", output_path)
message("  comprehensive_range_summary included in module6_output")
```

**Module 6 Complete!**

Results saved to: `r output_path`

---

**Session Info:**

```{r session_info, echo=FALSE}
sessionInfo()
```
